name: Eyal Barkan
description:
'''Eyal Barkan''' ([[Hebrew]]: אייל ברקן, born in [[1970]] in [[Haifa]]) is an [[Israelis|Israeli]] [[trance music|trance]]/[[nitzhonot]] producer.  He has collaborated with Yahel.  In 2003 he was reported to be Israel's top-selling trance DJ, with sales of over 150,000 albums. His albums have reached gold status in Israel. His 1998 hit Good Morning Israel was the first trance song to achieve platinum status in Israel.

His 2003 album ''V.I.P.'' was released by [[Tiësto]]'s Black Hole Records. In 2003 it was reported that cell phone ringtones based on Barkan's music achieved 200,000 downloads in Europe.

Barkan has also released music with his brother Oren as the duo Reverse Psychology. In 2006 the brothers started Startcut, a video distribution company.
